name: Google Chrome vs Firefox
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Google Chrome gives you a stable, familiar experience that works well if your digital life is tied to Google or you use anything outside MacOS, while Firefox trades away a lot of extension support and website compatibility for privacy.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Firefox if privacy is your top concern and you are okay with less extension support and occasional site issues; stick with Chrome otherwise.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Google Chrome vs Opera

name: Comet Browser vs Arc Browser
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Arc Browser rethinks what a browser can be and leads the pack, while Comet Browser stands out only for being the only truly free agentic browser with assistant features for researching and shopping.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Comet Browser if you need free agent mode for research or shopping; choose Arc Browser for a new experience that sets the standard.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Comet Browser vs Brave
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Comet Browser lets you use an assistant to handle tabs, emails, your calendar, and web navigation for free, while Brave focuses on privacy and crypto features that can be a hassle if you just want something simple.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Brave if you care most about privacy and crypto; otherwise, Comet Browser is the easier pick, especially for research and shopping.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Comet Browser vs Google Chrome
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Comet Browser gives you a free assistant that can handle your tabs, emails, calendar, and even browse the web for you, which Chrome cannot do.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Google Chrome if you want something stable and familiar or if your stuff is tied to Google and you are not on Mac.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Comet Browser vs Chromium

name: Comet Browser vs Edge

name: Comet Browser vs Firefox

name: Comet Browser vs Opera

tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper lets you run your entire Gmail inbox and calendar right inside the CRM, while Close forces you into its phone system and falls short on integration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Close if your team is all about high-volume calling and you need the autodialer more than anything; otherwise, stick with Copper.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs folk
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We love Copper & folk! We recommend folk for teams who are new to using a CRM (perhaps have never used one before and want something super simple and quick to setup).","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We recommend Copper for teams with more established processes who are looking to automate their processes and integrate their CRM with other third party software.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs GoHighLevel
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper gives you deep Google Workspace integration so you can manage Gmail right inside the CRM, which GoHighLevel does not match.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Avoid GoHighLevel and use Copper if you want a CRM that actually works well with Google tools.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs HubSpot
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you're a small-medium sized team, we recommend using Copper over HubSpot (especially if you're just looking for a CRM and don't need HubSpot's marketing automation features). Copper has an infinitely better user experience and is more affordable in the long-run.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs Monday
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper lets you handle your Gmail inbox and Google Workspace tools right inside the app, while Monday tries to do everything and ends up feeling unfocused.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Monday if you want an all-in-one tool and don't care about deep Google Workspace integration; otherwise, pick Copper.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs Notion
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper lets you fully manage Gmail and Google Workspace from inside its CRM, which Notion cannot match for email and calendar integration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Notion if you just need team docs, but pick Copper if you want deep Google Workspace support.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs Pipedrive
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper is the best CRM on the market for Google Workspace, so if you're using Google, there is no question that Copper is the best CRM for you 👌","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We recommend Pipedrive if you are using Microsoft 365.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs Salesforce
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper is a better CRM for small-medium sized teams (2–500 employees), while Salesforce should only be considered by enterprise teams (500–1000+ employees). If you're a small team, trust us, you do not need Salesforce.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs Streak
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Streak CRM is built into Gmail and is super convenient, but when we tried it we found it frustrating over time—it slows down Gmail, data can get unstructured and messy, which results in bad CRM habits. It's more like a glorified spreadsheet than a true CRM.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper CRM, on the other hand, is structured for scale, and builds a better foundation for businesses.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Copper vs Wonderly
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Copper is more of a traditional CRM, especially for teams already running their sales process through Google Workspace. It is built to help you manage contacts, emails, deals, and relationships.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Wonderly is a broader system built for trade and service businesses. Instead of just helping you manage leads once they come in, it helps with the front end too: your website, ads, lead capture, follow-up, and the operational side of turning those leads into closed jobs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Customer.io vs Mailchimp
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Customer.io lets you build segmented onboarding flows and manage communications visually, while Mailchimp buries you in confusing options and pushes templates that hurt engagement.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Customer.io if you need visual flows and Slack integration, otherwise skip Mailchimp and look for a simpler email tool.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Customer.io vs Reply
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Customer.io gives you clear control over segmented onboarding and customer communications with smooth Slack integration, while Reply's unreliable CRM syncing and shallow integrations force you into constant workarounds.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Reply if you never need to sync with a CRM or care about pricing surprises; otherwise, Customer.io is the safer pick.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Deel vs QuickBooks Online
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Deel handles global payroll, contracts, and compliance smoothly in over 150 countries, while QuickBooks Online only really fits US accounting and struggles with payroll or HR.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use QuickBooks Online if you need US-focused accounting; use Deel for anything involving international hiring or payroll.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Deel vs Rippling
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Deel streamlines hiring, payroll, and HR across 150 countries, making global workforce management easy, while Rippling focuses on large enterprises wanting payroll, HR, IT, finance, and spend tools in one place.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rippling if you need its all-in-one approach for a big company; choose Deel if you want hassle-free global hiring and compliance.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Arc Browser
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser adds a modern AI chatbot at its core while keeping the exceptional sidebar, tab management, and profiles from Arc Browser, which Arc itself does not mention having the AI integration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Arc Browser if you want the original browser that's rethinking the category, but Dia Browser gives you Arc's best features plus baked-in AI.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Brave
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you the slick features from Arc plus an AI chatbot built in, while Brave is all about strict privacy, ad blocking, and crypto tools that can feel like work to manage.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Brave if you really care about privacy and crypto and are willing to deal with seed phrases and data risks; otherwise, pick Dia Browser.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Google Chrome
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you smarter tab management, a standout sidebar, and built in AI chatbot features that Chrome does not offer.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Google Chrome if you rely on Google services and are not on Mac, otherwise Dia Browser is the clear pick for new features and experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Chromium
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you all the standout features from Arc plus a built-in AI chatbot, while Chromium just offers the basic foundation without those perks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Chromium if you want to tinker with the raw browser code; otherwise, stick with Dia Browser for a full experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Comet Browser
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia has a significantly better tab management experience compared Comet, so it's easier to actually organize and get through work.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"But when it comes to sheer AI browser functionality, Comet has the full power of Perplexity (free agent mode), while Dia more relies just on AI chat in the sidebar and is a wrapper for OpenAI and others.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Edge
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you all the best features from Arc like its sidebar and tab management, plus a built in AI chatbot, while Edge just wraps ChatGPT in some extra menus.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Edge if you have no other choice because Dia Browser is simply stronger for AI and daily browsing.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Firefox
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser brings all the standout features of Arc plus a built-in AI chatbot, while Firefox trades off extension support and site compatibility for privacy.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Firefox if privacy is your top concern; otherwise, Dia Browser is the clear pick for most people.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Opera
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser brings over Arc's standout sidebar, tab management, and profiles, but adds a modern AI chatbot at its core, while Opera mainly copies Arc's onboarding and features but feels unchanged aside from flashy animations.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Opera if you have a specific attachment, otherwise Dia Browser is the clear pick for a smarter, more modern experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Safari
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser brings the standout features of Arc with a modern AI chatbot at its core, while Safari sticks to a familiar but dull experience and misses the advanced features and enjoyable UX Dia offers.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Safari if you care most about stability and syncing across Apple devices and dislike new features; otherwise, Dia Browser is the clear pick.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Shift
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser brings Arc's standout sidebar, tab management, and profiles plus an AI chatbot, while Shift just feels like a Chrome add-on.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Shift if you need its account switching, but Dia Browser is the clear pick for a modern, feature-rich experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Sidekick
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you all the standout Arc Browser features plus a built in AI chatbot, while Sidekick feels outdated and clunky by comparison.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Sidekick if you have a specific need for it, since Dia Browser is a clear upgrade in every way mentioned.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs SigmaOS
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you all the best features from Arc like the sidebar and tab management, plus built in AI chat, while SigmaOS mostly copies Arc features and charges for them.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use SigmaOS if you specifically want what YC is backing, otherwise pick Dia Browser for the full feature set without extra cost.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Vivaldi
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser gives you all the standout features from Arc with a built in modern AI chatbot, while Vivaldi only makes sense if you absolutely need to move every button because you dislike standard layouts.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Stick with Dia Browser unless you are obsessed with customizing every detail, in which case Vivaldi is the only reason to switch.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dia Browser vs Zen Browser
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dia Browser delivers Arc's standout sidebar and tab management plus integrated AI, while Zen Browser copies some features but runs into extension issues and site bugs due to its Firefox base.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Stick with Dia Browser unless you specifically want to avoid Chromium, since Zen Browser trades away compatibility and stability.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dialpad vs Aircall
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"These are both VoIP solutions that we recommend, and have used in our own business.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"The distinction for Dialpad vs Aircall ultimately come down to product vs integration. While Dialpad has integrations and an API, unless you're using one of the big CRMs like Salesforce/HubSpot, it doesn't have the best integration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Aircall on the other hand has more native integrations with more CRMs, but not as great of a UX.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We personally use Dialpad and have a custom integration with Copper as we value the UX it has over Aircall, but would recommend most just go with Aircall instead.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dialpad vs Close
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dialpad gives you a modern VoIP setup with handy AI features and a mobile app that feels like texting, making it easy for business calls or networking, while Close forces you into its built-in VoIP and pricey autodialer.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Close if your sales team is all about high-volume phone outreach and will pay extra for the autodialer; otherwise, pick Dialpad.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dialpad vs JustCall
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dialpad gives you a modern business phone experience with reliable features and a mobile app that feels like SMS, while JustCall has annoying issues with CRM integrations and logging.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use JustCall if you just want a cheap, simple calling tool and do not care about reliable CRM workflows; otherwise, pick Dialpad.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dialpad vs RingCentral
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dialpad gives you a modern VoIP experience with built-in AI and a mobile app that feels like SMS, unlike RingCentral which feels outdated and is hard to use.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use RingCentral if you are a huge enterprise that cares more about stability than usability or innovation; otherwise, stick with Dialpad.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dub Links vs Bitly
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dub Links gives you real-time tracking, reliable analytics, and a modern interface, while Bitly feels outdated and limits key features.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Bitly if you have a specific reason, since Dub Links is just much easier and more useful day to day.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dub Links vs Short.io
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dub Links gives you real-time tracking, reliable analytics, and clear dashboards, while Short.io only handles basic redirects and has messy reporting.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Short.io if you have no other option; choose Dub Links for much better tracking, analytics, and usability.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dub Partners vs Cello
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dub Partners nails reliability, fraud protection, and user experience for affiliate programs, while Cello is really just for running peer-to-peer referrals inside your product.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Cello if you want users referring friends from within your product; use Dub Partners for anything involving creators, influencers, or publishers.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dub Partners vs FirstPromoter
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dub Partners nails reliability, fraud protection, and user experience while FirstPromoter signals to top partners that you are cutting corners.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use FirstPromoter if you do not care about attracting the best partners or having automatic payouts and accurate tracking.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dub Partners vs Impact

name: Dub Partners vs PartnerStack
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"While we're fans of both Dub Partners and PartnerStack (we genuinely believe they're the 2 leading affiliate tracking platforms), Dub Partners is a newer product that understands links and attribution at the core, so it gives us far more data than PartnerStack on what content is actually leading to the largest accounts. It's also priced better as well, and if you were to pick only 1, we'd recommend Dub Partners.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Dub Partners vs Rewardful
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Dub Partners nails reliability and fraud protection, gives you clear insight into what drives revenue, and automates payouts and tax compliance, while Rewardful leaves you chasing payments and your affiliates lost and frustrated.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rewardful if you have no other choice; choose Dub Partners for a smoother experience for you and your affiliates.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Edge vs Chromium

name: Edge vs Sidekick
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Edge pulls in some AI features through Copilot, but it's mostly just a wrapper for ChatGPT and doesn't stand out much, while Sidekick's productivity features feel outdated and the design is lacking even compared to other browsers.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Stick with Edge if you have to pick between these two, as Sidekick does not deliver on its productivity claims or user experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Expensify vs QuickBooks Online
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"QuickBooks Online covers accounting and works best for US businesses, especially if your accountant already uses QuickBooks, but its expense management is clunky compared to Expensify.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Expensify if you just need basic receipt forwarding, since QuickBooks Online is better overall for accounting needs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Expensify vs Rippling
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Expensify handles receipt forwarding if you need something basic, but Rippling is built for large enterprises wanting one tool for payroll, HR, IT, finance, and expenses.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Expensify if you just need simple receipt forwarding; choose Rippling if you're at a large company and want everything in one system.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Fantastical vs Google Calendar

name: Fathom Analytics vs Google Analytics
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Fathom Analytics gives you fast, privacy-focused stats without slowing down your site, while Google Analytics brings confusing setup, slowdowns, and privacy headaches.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Google Analytics if you need deep Google Ads integration for conversion tracking; otherwise, stick with Fathom Analytics.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

Edge tries to stand out with built-in AI features but does not surpass alternatives and lacks a real advantage over Firefox.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Edge if you specifically want Microsoft's AI integrations; otherwise, Firefox is the more privacy-focused pick.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Firefox vs Toby

name: folk vs Airtable

name: folk vs Close
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"folk makes managing contacts and relationships easy and feels like upgrading from a spreadsheet, while Close forces you into its own dialer and gets frustrating unless nonstop phone calls are your thing.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Close if your team is all about high-volume phone outreach and really needs that autodialer, otherwise stick with folk.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: folk vs GoHighLevel

name: folk vs HubSpot
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"folk feels like a spreadsheet for managing contacts and makes it easy for individuals or small teams new to CRMs, while HubSpot is expensive and has a clunky user experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use HubSpot if you have a specific reason, otherwise pick folk for a smoother and more affordable start.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: folk vs Monday

name: folk vs Salesforce

tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Framer gives you a fast way to make great looking marketing sites or blogs with slick animations and a Figma-like feel, while Webflow is just described as good for getting a website MVP up quickly.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Webflow if you just want a basic MVP, but pick Framer if you care about looks and want a simple, polished site without code.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Framer vs Webstudio
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Framer makes it easy to build stylish marketing sites or blogs with slick animations and a visual interface, while Webstudio gives you open-source control and lets you connect your own database with modern code.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Webstudio if you want open source and backend control; stick with Framer for fast, eye-catching sites without coding.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Front vs Gmail
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Front lets teams handle email, social, and chat in one inbox, which Gmail does not, but only matters if your support actually runs on all those channels.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Front if your team truly relies on multiple channels every day; stick with Gmail as your basic email if you just need a standard inbox.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Front vs Gorgias
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Front brings email, social, and chat into one inbox, which helps teams juggling support across those channels, while Gorgias focuses on tight integration with eCommerce tools like Shopify.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Front if your team depends on handling email, social, and chat together; stick with Gorgias for eCommerce support.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Front vs Intercom
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Front handles email, social, and chat messages in one inbox, which works best if you really need to manage multiple channels, while Intercom is focused on live chat and can overwhelm small teams with instant response pressure and high costs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Intercom if you have a big team ready for nonstop chat and can cover the price; otherwise, stick with Front.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Front vs Missive
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Front combines email, social, and chat into one inbox, which helps if you really rely on all those channels for support, while Missive focuses on keeping team email and message collaboration organized and easy to delegate.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Front if your support depends on juggling social and chat with email; choose Missive if you mostly need smooth team email management.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Front vs Outlook
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Front combines email, social, and chat into one inbox, which Outlook does not, but only makes sense if handling multiple channels is central to your team.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outlook if you are locked into Microsoft 365 and do not need to manage support from social or chat alongside email.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Front vs Zendesk
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Front gives teams an easy way to manage email, social, and chat in one inbox, while Zendesk feels clunky and frustrating for users.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Zendesk if you have strict enterprise needs; otherwise, pick Front for a much better day to day experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Fyxer AI vs Mailman
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Fyxer AI just repackages basic AI features you likely already have in your business software, while Mailman actually helps by batching emails and letting you approve new senders to cut distractions.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Fyxer AI if you want simple AI features sold as something bigger, but pick Mailman if you need real control over when and how emails hit your inbox.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Gmail vs Outlook

That said, if you really want the best scheduling tool, then check out our Cal.com review.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Google Calendar vs SavvyCal
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Google Calendar anchors your scheduling and is essential for business use, while SavvyCal offers a nicer interface but falls short for complex needs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use SavvyCal if you care most about a personal scheduling feel; stick with Google Calendar if you want reliability and core functionality.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Google Drive vs Box
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Box gives you strict permissions, audit trails, and compliance, which Google Drive does not, so it's for regulated data or tight control.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Google Drive if you want simple file storage and do not need the extra controls or compliance features that Box offers.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Google Meet vs Dialpad Meetings
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Google Meet avoids hassles since people know it and it connects easily, while Dialpad Meetings just causes confusion and extra steps.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Dialpad Meetings if you're fine dealing with friction and workarounds, but Google Meet is the clear pick for most teams.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Google Workspace vs Microsoft 365
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Google Workspace connects easily with almost every other app, while Microsoft 365 feels clunky and locks you into its own ecosystem.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Microsoft 365 if you have no choice; Google Workspace is what you want for flexibility and a modern team experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Google Workspace vs Superhuman Suite
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Google Workspace integrates easily with almost every software, making it much friendlier with outside tools than Superhuman Suite.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Superhuman Suite if you specifically want its bundled tools, but Google Workspace is the clear pick for working with modern apps.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Gorgias vs Gmail
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Gorgias nails eCommerce help desks with deep Shopify and tool integration, while Gmail just gives you a basic, cluttered mail client.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Gmail as the base for your email and layer something else on top if your inbox is busy; skip Gorgias unless you run an eCommerce company.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Gorgias vs Intercom
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Gorgias gives eCommerce companies tight integration with top eCommerce tools like Shopify, while Intercom adds high costs and demands instant responses that can overwhelm small teams.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Intercom if you have several full time staff for live chat and can handle the extra cost; otherwise stick with Gorgias.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Gorgias vs Missive
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Gorgias handles eCommerce support best by connecting deeply with tools like Shopify, while Missive focuses on team email and message collaboration across platforms.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Gorgias if you need help desk features tied to eCommerce tools; stick with Missive if your team needs to manage shared inboxes and messages together.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Gorgias vs Plain

name: Gorgias vs Zendesk
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Gorgias nails it for eCommerce teams with deep integration to top tools like Shopify, while Zendesk feels old and frustrating to use.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Zendesk if your enterprise team has a specific reason, otherwise pick Gorgias for a smoother experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Granola vs Amie
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Granola records every meeting automatically and lets you ask questions during calls, while Amie just takes notes without needing a meeting bot.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Amie if you only want simple AI notes and never need full meeting recall or mid-call help that Granola gives.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Granola vs Cluely
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Granola is incredibly light-weight, runs in the background, and gives you perfect recollection of all your calls, only presenting itself when needed.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Cluely goes the direction of getting way too involved in what is being said, with an attempt to proactively research and suggest what to say, but it fails to deliver in the marketing promise.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Granola vs Dialpad Meetings
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Granola gives you a full record of every meeting and live help during calls, while Dialpad Meetings just makes joining and integrating harder.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Dialpad Meetings if you are fine with extra hassle and missing the meeting memory Granola provides.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Granola vs Gong

name: Harvest vs Rize
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Harvest makes tracking client and project hours simple and lets you manage budgets and invoicing in one place, while Rize struggles if you need anything beyond solo productivity or want accurate team or client tracking.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rize if you just want personal productivity reports; choose Harvest for any work with clients, projects, or teams.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Harvest vs Timely
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Harvest makes it easy to log hours for client and project work, manage budgets, and handle invoicing without drowning in confusing details, while Timely just leaves you sorting through a mess of browser activity and URLs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Timely if you want to manually sift through data, but choose Harvest if you want clear project tracking without hassle.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Harvest vs Toggl
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Harvest lets you track time by project or client and handle invoicing in one place, while Toggl adds steps and leaves billing out.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Toggl if you never need to invoice or log time for clients or projects, otherwise choose Harvest.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Front
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Help Scout keeps customer service personal and avoids making people feel like ticket numbers, while Front pulls email, social, and chat into one inbox for teams needing all those channels together.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Front if your support relies heavily on managing email, social, and chat in one place; stick with Help Scout for a more customer centric approach.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Gmail
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Help Scout gives your customers a personal touch and avoids making them feel like just a ticket, while Gmail feels cluttered and is aimed at casual users.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Gmail if you just need a basic email foundation, but choose Help Scout if good customer service matters to your team.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Gorgias
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Help Scout puts customer service first so your customers feel valued, while Gorgias is focused on eCommerce integrations like Shopify.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Gorgias if your main need is connecting with eCommerce tools; stick with Help Scout if customer care matters most.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Intercom
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Help Scout is our favorite help desk software (it's what we use) for small-medium sized teams. While it has a live-chat feature, it is a secondary feature, while for Intercom live-chat is at the core of what they do.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you are a small team, we don't recommend having live-chat as you'll find yourself glued to your computer 😄","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Typically a lot of startups and SaaS companies use Intercom.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Missive
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Help Scout makes your customers feel like people instead of ticket numbers, unlike Missive which is focused on team email collaboration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Missive if your main need is organizing team discussions and managing messages from several platforms in one inbox.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Outlook
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Help Scout gives your team a shared inbox that helps you treat customers like people instead of ticket numbers, unlike Outlook.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outlook if you are tied to Microsoft 365, but Help Scout is the pick if you want customer service that actually feels personal.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Plain
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Looking for a more traditional and customer-centric help desk? Help Scout has done an incredible job at not just doing this, but having their customers magnify this.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"On the other hand, if you're a deeply product-focused company using Slack for shared customer channels, where support and engineering tickets are often started from Slack, Plain is building specifically for you.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Help Scout vs Zendesk
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Most companies that use Zendesk do so because they're forced to due to enterprise scale, not because they want to. We've still yet to meet a single person in customer success that enjoys using Zendesk.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"So if you're a startup or small business, be glad that you get to actually use an enjoyable help desk like Help Scout—in-fact, your customer success team will appreciate you for it.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Hey vs Inbox Zero
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Hey relies on a less modern SMPT connection that causes major limitations for business use, while Inbox Zero helps you label, organize, and bulk unsubscribe from emails if you use Google Workspace or Microsoft.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Inbox Zero if you need email organizing and unsubscribing on Google or Microsoft; skip Hey for professional email entirely.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Hey vs Outlook
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Hey struggles with work email because it relies on an old SMPT connection that brings a lot of limitations, unlike Outlook. Outlook is standard for Microsoft 365 and avoids those issues, so skip Hey for professional use and stick with Outlook if these are your only two choices.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Hey vs Skiff
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Hey works for email but has major limitations for business use, while Skiff is shut down and no longer available.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Hey if you must pick between these, since Skiff cannot be used at all.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: HubSpot vs Airtable
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Airtable gives you a flexible, user-friendly database for business tasks like replacing Google Sheets, while HubSpot gets expensive fast and has a poor user experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use HubSpot if you specifically need a CRM and can't use anything else; choose Airtable for almost any other structured business data need.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: HubSpot vs Asana
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Asana handles complex project management with lots of features, but it takes serious setup and ongoing effort, while HubSpot quickly gets expensive and has a clunky user experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Asana if you have a big team and a dedicated project manager; skip HubSpot for smaller teams since better, cheaper options exist.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: HubSpot vs Close
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"HubSpot costs a lot very quickly and the user experience is underwhelming, but Close only makes sense if your team is all-in on high-volume phone outreach and willing to pay a premium for its autodialer, otherwise you quickly run into frustrating limits.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Close if your sales team lives for that built-in autodialer; use HubSpot over Close for anything else, especially if you want more CRM flexibility.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: HubSpot vs Monday
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"HubSpot gives you strong marketing automation but its CRM feels more like a teaser to get you paying for expensive extras, while Monday tries to do everything and ends up not nailing anything.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use HubSpot if you want marketing automation above all else; skip Monday unless you have no other choice.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: HubSpot vs Notion
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"HubSpot gets expensive quickly and the user experience is not great, while Notion is only good for team docs and struggles with everything else.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Notion if you just need a place for team docs; skip HubSpot and Notion for anything beyond that.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}
