name: Motion vs Todoist
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Motion is a full project management tool while Todoist is more built for basic task management. While Todoist has a team features, it's missing collaborative features like task dependencies and longer-term planning views like Gantt Charts and team workload.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Motion vs Trello
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"You can't truly compare Motion and Trello. Trello is a relatively barebones way to manage tasks via a Kanban view, whereas Kanban views in Motion are just one type of view and only just a small feature of Motion. Motion is much better suited for busy professionals and teams who have full projects to manage (not just simple tasks here-and-there).","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Motion vs Vimcal
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Motion and Vimcal both have calendar at the core but are built for two totally different types of people. You can manage tasks and even projects within Motion, while with Vimcal, there are no task management capabilities. Vimcal is simply a calendar with meeting scheduling features.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Motion vs Wrike
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Wrike is more of an enterprise project management software (1000+ employees), while Motion is way better suited for small-medium sized teams.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Motion is innovating in the PM space as it's built with a calendar at the core which is a game-changer because it actually takes into account your entire teams schedule (meetings AND work) and will proactively let you know if you are at risk of missing a deadline.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Calendar vs Akiflow
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Calendar gives you a nice looking free calendar for iOS and feels cleaner for basic scheduling than Akiflow, but don't bother trying to manage tasks in it outside of kludgy Notion database items.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Akiflow if you really need to pull tasks from lots of places, but expect overwhelm fast and skip it for simple calendar needs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Calendar (Cron) vs Fantastical
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Calendar gives you a cleaner, free calendar experience than Fantastical, but skip using it for managing tasks or Notion database items.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Fantastical if you have very basic family needs, since Notion Calendar is the better pick for almost everyone else.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Calendar (Cron) vs Google Calendar
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Calendar gives you a nicer looking calendar for iOS if you just want something free and simpler than Google Calendar, but it is not available for Outlook and gets messy if you try to use it for tasks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Google Calendar if you need something for business or as a base for other calendar tools, since it is the standard everyone builds on.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Vimcal vs Notion Calendar (Cron)
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Vimcal is better if you're looking for a sleek and fast calendar, and want a way to schedule meetings with your team while on-the-go. Notion Calendar is better for those who are already using Notion since you can add database items to your calendar (because... why not I suppose? 😅)","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"That said, none of these tools are our top picks, there's some awesome calendars in our ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733507737","type":"link","fields":{"doc":{"value":48,"relationTo":"best"},"newTab":false,"linkType":"internal","url":"/best/calendar"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"best calendar apps","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":" list.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Mail vs Gmail
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Important: ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail shut down September 22nd, 2026.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":1},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail formerly gave you a cleaner interface, lets you use custom views, and makes it easy to reference Notion docs while Gmail feels cluttered and slow.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Gmail if you just need a basic backbone for email or already have Google Workspace, but Notion Mail is the better daily experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Mail vs Hey
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Important: ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail shut down September 22nd, 2026.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":1},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail formerly gave a smoother experience than Gmail with extras like custom views and pulling in Notion docs, while Hey has major issues for professional use because of its outdated SMPT setup.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Hey if you have a specific reason, but for most work email needs, pick Notion Mail.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Mail vs Outlook
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Important: ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail shut down September 22nd, 2026.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":1},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail formerly gave you a smoother experience than Outlook by letting you reference Notion docs and set up custom views in email.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outlook if you are locked into Microsoft 365; otherwise, Notion Mail is the clear upgrade.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Mail vs Skiff
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Important: ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail shut down September 22nd, 2026.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":1},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail formerly gave you a nicer Gmail experience with custom views and lets you pull in Notion docs, while Skiff is shut down after being acquired by Notion.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Skiff if you already have it for some reason, but Notion Mail is the one that actually works now.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Mail vs Spark
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Important: ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail shut down September 22nd, 2026.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":1},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail formerly gave you a nicer interface and lets you pull in Notion docs while emailing, which Spark does not.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Spark if you want something free and a bit faster than Gmail or Outlook, but Notion Mail feels nicer to use day to day.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Notion Mail vs Tatem
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Important: ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":1,"version":1},{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail shut down September 22nd, 2026.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":1},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Mail formerly gave you a smoother experience with some extra features like custom views and referencing Notion docs, which Tatem lacks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Tatem if you absolutely need its minimal setup, but Notion Mail is the clear pick since Tatem is no longer being maintained.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Novo vs Brex
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Novo works for basic checking if you just need to move money around, but you'll hit limits fast if your business grows, while Brex has a reputation for abruptly shutting down accounts and many left after Capital One bought them.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Novo if you're a freelancer or very small business with simple needs; skip Brex unless you have no other option.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: OpenClaw vs Claude
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Claude actually helps with work, coding, and writing, while OpenClaw is only worth touching if you want to tinker and don't care about security.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use OpenClaw if you're just playing around on a spare device; otherwise, pick Claude for anything you care about.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: OpenClaw vs Sintra AI
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"OpenClaw gives you control over your computer with an AI agent, but it's a big security risk and not safe for anything important, while Sintra AI is made for business owners who want super simple AI help without learning prompts.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use OpenClaw if you're a hobbyist experimenting on a device you don't care about; stick with Sintra AI if you just want easy business ideas and have no technical background.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Opera vs Chromium
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Opera gives you a complete experience with features and polish, while Chromium just offers the bare code and lacks those conveniences.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Chromium if you specifically want to tinker with the underlying browser tech; otherwise, stick with Opera.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Opera vs Edge
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Opera offers a refreshed look with new onboarding and flashy animations, while Edge leans on AI features like Copilot that mostly just wrap ChatGPT.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Edge if you really want basic AI tools in your browser; otherwise, Opera is the more worthwhile pick.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Opera vs Sidekick
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Opera tries to stay current with new looks and features, but underneath it's still the same browser, while Sidekick feels dated and its supposed productivity perks are now standard elsewhere.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Opera if you want something familiar with a bit of polish; skip Sidekick since its design and features lag behind even Opera.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Opera vs Vivaldi
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Opera gives you a modern look and smoother onboarding, while Vivaldi only stands out if you obsess over customizing every button.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Vivaldi if you truly need extreme customization; otherwise, stick with Opera for a more current experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ordinal vs Buffer
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ordinal saves you time with reliable scheduling and auto-engagement that boosts every LinkedIn and X post, while Buffer stands out only for Instagram posting and its generous free tier.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Buffer if Instagram is your main focus or you need a free plan; otherwise, Ordinal is the clear pick for managing and engaging on LinkedIn and X.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ordinal vs Hootsuite
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ordinal makes scheduling simple and reliable, with auto-engagement that boosts every post and a content drafting tool that saves real time, while Hootsuite feels clunky, overwhelming, and overpriced for most needs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Hootsuite if you are an enterprise with complex management and need deep analytics, otherwise use Ordinal.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ordinal vs Metricool
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ordinal nails simple UX, reliable scheduling, and auto-engagement that boosts posts right away, which Metricool just doesn't deliver.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Metricool if you can't use Ordinal, since Metricool's limitations pushed us to switch away for Instagram, X, and LinkedIn posting.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ordinal vs Typefully
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ordinal saves you time by reliably scheduling posts and auto-boosting them with team engagement, while Typefully focuses on analytics and automations like Auto-Retweet for X and LinkedIn.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Typefully if you care most about analytics or X automations; pick Ordinal if you want hands-off posting and more team-driven engagement.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outfunnel vs Make
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Outfunnel syncs your sales and marketing data across tools and your CRM without needing technical skills, while Make expects you to handle APIs and complex setups.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Make if you're technical and want to build advanced automations; otherwise, stick with Outfunnel for easy integration and lead tracking.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outfunnel vs Reply
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Outfunnel actually syncs your sales and marketing data with your CRM and logs activity from tools like Calendly, while Reply's integrations are unreliable and make you build messy workarounds just to get your data out.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Reply if you never need real CRM integration and don't care about surprise costs, otherwise pick Outfunnel.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outfunnel vs Surfe
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Outfunnel syncs sales and marketing data from many tools into your CRM and tracks lead engagement, while Surfe only connects LinkedIn with your CRM for easy lead management inside LinkedIn.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Surfe if you spend most of your time finding leads on LinkedIn; otherwise, stick with Outfunnel for broader integration and tracking.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outfunnel vs Whalesync
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Outfunnel gives your sales team a complete view of the customer journey by syncing CRM data, tracking engagement, and logging activity, while Whalesync is all about reliable 2-way syncing across apps like Webflow, Airtable, Notion, and HubSpot.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Whalesync if you need complex bidirectional sync with those specific apps, but stick with Outfunnel for CRM-focused automation and lead tracking.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outfunnel vs Zapier
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Outfunnel syncs sales and marketing data to your CRM and tracks lead engagement in depth, while Zapier connects apps and automates workflows more generally.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outfunnel if you need deep CRM integration for sales and marketing; choose Zapier for broader app connections and automation.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outlook vs Inbox Zero
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Inbox Zero helps you label and organize emails and bulk unsubscribe from newsletters, while Outlook is just the standard Microsoft 365 email client.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outlook if you are already locked into Microsoft 365, but use Inbox Zero if you want more help managing your inbox.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Outlook vs Skiff
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Outlook works as the standard email client inside Microsoft 365, while Skiff was a privacy-focused email client that got shut down.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outlook since Skiff is no longer available.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PandaDoc vs DocuSign
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PandaDoc gives you a cleaner, easier way to handle proposals, contracts, eSignatures, and payments in one place than DocuSign.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use DocuSign if you are at a large business that needs enterprise features, otherwise stick with PandaDoc for a more user-friendly experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PandaDoc vs Ironclad
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PandaDoc makes creating and managing proposals, contracts, and payments simple and user-friendly, while Ironclad focuses on maximum security for contracts and eSignatures.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Ironclad if you work in a highly data-sensitive field; otherwise, PandaDoc is the easier and more modern choice.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PartnerStack vs Cello
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PartnerStack gives B2B SaaS companies a single login and handles payouts, which Cello does not, making PartnerStack far better for affiliate partnerships.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Cello if you need a simple way to manage peer-to-peer referrals inside your product and do not care about traditional affiliate programs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PartnerStack vs FirstPromoter
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PartnerStack gives you a single login and handles payouts smoothly, while FirstPromoter signals to top partners that you are cutting corners.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use FirstPromoter if you have no other choice; choose PartnerStack if you want to attract quality partners and run things easily.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PartnerStack vs Impact
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PartnerStack gives B2B SaaS companies exactly what they need, like single login and payouts, while Impact misses the mark for software.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Impact if you are running big B2C eCommerce or coupon programs; for SaaS, always go with PartnerStack.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PartnerStack vs Rewardful
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PartnerStack gives affiliates a single login and handles payouts, while Rewardful forces users to juggle many accounts and manual payments.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rewardful if you have no other choice; PartnerStack is much easier for affiliates and saves you from payment headaches.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: PartnerStack vs Tapfiliate
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"PartnerStack gives affiliates one login for everything and handles payouts, while Tapfiliate makes partners manage a separate login they forget.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Tapfiliate if you have no other choice; PartnerStack is better for you and your partners in every way described.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Payload vs Directus
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"While Directus has been around far longer, ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733508025","type":"link","fields":{"doc":{"value":31,"relationTo":"review"},"url":"/apps/payload","newTab":false,"linkType":"internal"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Payload","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":" has come out of the gate with an incredibly modern stack, focused heavily on NextJS and full open-source modularity.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We've been seeing huge companies like Disney and Bugatti moving over to Payload, and we believe that they are going to disrupt the space over the next 2–3 years.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"That said, if you're looking for the most stable option that has been tried/true/tested, you're probably better off going with ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733508027","type":"link","fields":{"doc":{"value":32,"relationTo":"review"},"url":"/apps/directus","newTab":false,"linkType":"internal"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Directus","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":" (for now).","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Airtable
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive handles sales-focused CRM tasks for small and medium teams, while Airtable is not recommended for CRM use.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Airtable if you need a flexible database to replace Google Sheets or forms; stick with Pipedrive for CRM, especially with Microsoft 365.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Asana
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive nails sales-focused CRM for small or medium teams, especially if you use Microsoft 365, while Asana is built for complex project management but demands serious setup and maintenance.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Asana if you have a large team, a dedicated project manager, and budget; skip it for simple needs or quick adoption and stick with Pipedrive for sales-focused CRM.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Close
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive nails sales-focused CRM for small and medium teams and is especially good if you're on Microsoft 365, while Close forces you into its pricey autodialer and limits flexibility.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Close if your team cares about high-volume phone outreach above everything else; otherwise, pick Pipedrive.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs GoHighLevel
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive focuses on being a good sales CRM for small to medium teams and is the one to use if you're on Microsoft 365, unlike GoHighLevel.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use GoHighLevel if you want to deal with a resold MLM scheme and software that is not actually recommended over Pipedrive.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs HubSpot
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We recommend Pipedrive over HubSpot, especially if you are a small–medium sized team (under 500 employees). Pipedrive has a superior user experience and is also more affordable in the long-run compared to HubSpot.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive is also more of a sales-focused CRM, whereas HubSpot is more of a marketing-focused CRM.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Monday
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive gives you a focused sales CRM that works well for small or medium teams, while Monday tries to do everything and ends up feeling unfocused.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Monday if you need an all-in-one tool and can live with compromises, but stick with Pipedrive if you're after a straightforward sales CRM.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Notion
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive handles sales-focused CRM for small and medium teams well, while Notion struggles outside basic team docs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Notion if you just need a shared team knowledge base; choose Pipedrive if you want a CRM for Microsoft 365 users.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Salesforce
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive handles sales-focused CRM needs for small and medium teams and is what you should use if you're on Microsoft 365, while Salesforce is aimed at massive enterprise teams and isn't a fit for under 1000 employees.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Salesforce if you have an enterprise team of at least 1000 people; use Pipedrive for everyone else.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Pipedrive vs Streak
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Pipedrive handles sales-focused CRM tasks for small and medium teams smoothly, while Streak clutters up Gmail and feels like a messy spreadsheet.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Streak if you absolutely must run your CRM inside Google Sheets; otherwise, pick Pipedrive every time.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Plain vs Front
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you're a product-driven company looking for an API-focused and deeply integrated help desk, Plain is your best bet.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"On the other hand, if you're looking for a collaborative help desk that integrates into all of your support and social media channels, then Front would work better.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Plain vs Gmail
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Plain gives you a simple, API-first help desk with strong Slack and Linear integrations, while Gmail feels cluttered and slow for daily email.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Gmail if you need a basic email backbone for Google Workspace, but pick Plain if you want focused tools for a product or engineering team.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Plain vs Intercom
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Intercom is more known as being a live chat tool than a help desk even. So if you're looking to have a more chat-focused experience with your customers, Intercom is your best bet.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"On the other hand, if you're a product that has shared Slack connect channels with your customers and support often flows into Slack, then Plain has your back with an incredibly robust and deep Slack integration bringing everything to a single pane of glass.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Plain vs Missive
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Plain gives you a simple single inbox and tight integrations with Slack and Linear, while Missive focuses on handling emails and messages from multiple platforms with easy delegation and organized team discussions.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Plain if you care most about an API-first help desk and software tool integrations; stick with Missive for smoother team email and message collaboration.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Plain vs Outlook
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Plain gives product and engineering teams a simple, API-first help desk with deep Slack and Linear integrations, while Outlook is just the default email in Microsoft 365 and isn't recommended for fast-moving teams.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Outlook if you're locked into Microsoft 365; otherwise, Plain is the better fit for software-focused teams.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Plain vs Zendesk
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Plain gives you a simple single inbox and integrates tightly with Slack and Linear, while Zendesk feels clunky and frustrates customer success reps.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Zendesk if you are an enterprise team with a specific need, otherwise pick Plain for a better team and customer experience.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Polar vs Airwallex
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Polar handles all your sales tax and VAT for you, taking that off your plate, while Airwallex focuses on making cross-border payments easy and lets you keep money in multiple currencies.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Airwallex if you need to accept and spend money internationally in multiple currencies; stick with Polar if you want tax and VAT handled for you and are focused on growing a startup.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Polar vs Stripe
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Polar handles all your sales tax and VAT as your MOR, so you can just use it and focus on your business instead of tax hassles, while Stripe only becomes necessary once your SaaS startup hits $4–10M/ARR.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Stripe if you're a venture-backed SaaS startup doing $4–10M/ARR+, otherwise just use Polar.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Postmark vs Customer.io
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Postmark used to be great for transactional email, but since ActiveCampaign bought them, things have gone downhill compared to Customer.io.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Customer.io if you want to build segmented onboarding flows or send updates to tools like Slack, but skip it for full-featured email marketing.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Postmark vs Mailchimp
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Postmark used to deliver reliably for transactional emails but has gotten worse since ActiveCampaign took over, while Mailchimp makes sending even basic emails confusing and pushes templates that hurt engagement.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Skip Mailchimp unless you want frustration, and only use Postmark if you have no simpler alternative.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: QuickBooks Online vs Xero
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"QuickBooks Online nails US-specific accounting and US sales tax, while Xero is the better pick if you're outside the US.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use QuickBooks Online if you need something US-focused; choose Xero for accounting anywhere else.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ramp vs Airwallex
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you're looking for a global payment solution or your business is based outside of the US, ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733507662","type":"link","fields":{"doc":{"value":12,"relationTo":"review"},"newTab":false,"linkType":"internal","url":"/apps/airwallex"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Airwallex","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":" will be your best bet, otherwise we'd recommend ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733507706","type":"link","fields":{"doc":{"value":93,"relationTo":"review"},"newTab":false,"linkType":"internal","url":"/apps/ramp"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ramp","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":" if you're based in the US and looking for an expense management and virtual card solution.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ramp vs BILL Spend & Expense
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"You can't go wrong with either BILL S&E or Ramp for virtual credit cards and expense management.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"If you're a small business or have less cashflow, then BILL is your best bet as Ramp requires at-least 25K in your business bank (with your limit directly tied to this, so we really recommend you have $50–100K minimum in your account).","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"The most simple way to put it is, if you're a small business, use ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733507947","type":"link","fields":{"url":"https://efficient.link/r/bill","newTab":true,"linkType":"custom"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"BILL S&E","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":", if you're a startup with millions in the bank or mid-market, use ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733507947","type":"link","fields":{"url":"https://efficient.link/r/ramp","newTab":true,"linkType":"custom"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ramp","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":".","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

Even if Mercury had feature-parity, we would still use it alongside BILL or Ramp.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ramp vs Novo
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ramp fixes annoying expense reporting and card headaches for businesses with at least $25K in the bank, while Novo is just a simple checking account for freelancers or very small businesses and doesn't handle growth well.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Novo if you're a solo operator who just needs somewhere to park cash; use Ramp for anything bigger or if you hate clunky finance tools.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ramp vs QuickBooks Online
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ramp fixes messy expense reporting and corporate card hassles, while QuickBooks Online is clunky for expense management and payroll.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use QuickBooks Online if you need US-focused accounting and your accountant expects it; otherwise, Ramp is the clear pick for expense management.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Ramp vs Rippling
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"We find it tough to recommend Rippling Spend over Ramp or ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733508034","type":"link","fields":{"doc":{"value":130,"relationTo":"review"},"url":"/apps/bill","newTab":false,"linkType":"internal"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"BILL Spend & Expense","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":".","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"What we'd say here is, if you're a team that is already using Rippling for other areas of their business like Payroll, maybe ","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1},{"id":"1760733507736","type":"link","fields":{"url":"https://efficient.link/r/rippling","newTab":true,"linkType":"custom"},"format":"","indent":0,"version":3,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"give Rippling Spend a shot","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":"ltr"},{"mode":"normal","text":".","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Otherwise, you're probably better off with Ramp as it'll give a better UI/UX.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Relay vs Brex
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Relay gives you a way to organize your cash flow using separate buckets, while Brex is known for shutting down accounts unexpectedly.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Brex if you have no other options and are comfortable with the risk; otherwise, Relay is the safer pick for managing your business funds.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Relay vs Novo
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Relay works for smaller service-based business owners, while Novo is for very small businesses like freelancers or solopreneurs with $5K to $100K in the account.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Novo if you are a freelancer or solopreneur with a small account; otherwise, stick with Relay.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Relay vs Ramp
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ramp fixes annoying expense reports and streamlines corporate card use, while Relay focuses on helping owners organize cash flow using Profit First buckets.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Relay if you specifically want cash flow buckets, but choose Ramp for smoother expense management in any modern business.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Relay vs Rho
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Relay works for smaller service-based business owners, while Rho tries to do a lot but only does some things well.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Stick with Relay if you fit its target, and avoid Rho unless you need its extra features and can live with the tradeoffs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rewardful vs Cello
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rewardful creates headaches for affiliate programs since affiliates avoid logging in, you end up chasing payments, and nobody knows what content works, while Cello is not made for affiliates at all but shines for in-product peer-to-peer referrals.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Cello if you want simple referral links inside your product; skip Rewardful for affiliate programs entirely.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rewardful vs Impact
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rewardful creates constant headaches by making affiliates juggle dozens of accounts, leaves you sending endless PayPal payments, and gives affiliates no insight into what is working, while Impact fails to track reliably and is built for B2C eCommerce, not SaaS.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rewardful or Impact if you have no SaaS needs at all; skip both for SaaS and use Dub Partners instead.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rewardful vs Tapfiliate
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rewardful creates a mess for you and your affiliates, forcing everyone to juggle endless accounts and manual payments, while Tapfiliate just adds another login your partners will ignore.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Skip Rewardful unless you want constant headaches, but only use Tapfiliate if you have no other choice.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rho vs Brex
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rho covers banking and expense management in one place, but only some features actually work well, while Brex has a track record of randomly shutting down accounts and has lost users since Capital One took over.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rho if you need an all-in-one and can handle some weak spots; avoid Brex unless you are comfortable with sudden account closures.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rho vs Novo
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rho covers banking and expense management in one place and actually works well at least for some tasks, while Novo only fits very small businesses.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Novo if you are a freelancer or solopreneur with a small account; otherwise, pick Rho.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rho vs Ramp
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Ramp fixes annoying expense reporting and card hassles that Rho doesn't, and stands out for companies with $25K or more in the bank.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rho if Ramp's $25K requirement is a blocker, since Ramp is the clear pick for smoother business finances.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: RingCentral vs JustCall
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"RingCentral covers the basics and connects with many tools, but actually using it feels clunky and frustrating unless you have IT support, while JustCall is simpler but stumbles with unreliable CRM integrations and logging.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use JustCall if you want something cheap and can tolerate integration headaches; skip RingCentral unless you need stability and have IT help.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rippling vs QuickBooks Online
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rippling covers payroll, HR, IT, finance, and spend management for big companies, while QuickBooks Online is only strong for US accounting and is weak for payroll and expenses.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rippling if you run a large enterprise needing everything in one place; stick with QuickBooks Online if you want standard US accounting and your accountant prefers it.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rize vs Timely
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rize gives you easy reports on your own focus and habits, while Timely just floods you with confusing browser data you have to sort out.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Rize if you want solo productivity tracking; skip Timely entirely unless you want to dig through raw URLs.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Rize vs Toggl
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Rize tracks your time automatically and gives you nice reports about your own focus, but it can't handle client, project, or team tracking like Toggl tries to do.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Toggl if you need to log time for clients or projects, but skip it for invoicing or if you want something easy for team habits.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Routine vs Amie
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine tries to tie together daily planning with knowledge and project management but ends up feeling confusing, while Amie now focuses on being an AI notetaker and keeps its calendar and to-do features secondary.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Routine if you want everything mixed together, but choose Amie if you mainly want an AI notetaker without a meeting bot.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Routine vs Fantastical
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Routine tries to do too much by mixing daily planning with knowledge and project management, ending up confusing compared to Fantastical.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Fantastical if you want a basic personal or family calendar, but skip it for anything professional or if you want something modern.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Routine vs Google Calendar
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Google Calendar gives you a dependable base for scheduling, while Routine gets bogged down trying to do too many things and ends up confusing.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Routine if you want a cluttered mix of features, but stick with Google Calendar as your main tool and add something on top if you need more.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Routine vs Notion Calendar (Cron)
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Notion Calendar gives you a clean calendar for iOS and does better than Google Calendar if you just want a simple free calendar, while Routine tries to do too much with knowledge and project management and ends up confusing instead of helping you plan your day.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Routine if you want an all-in-one suite and don't mind a cluttered experience, but pick Notion Calendar if you just need a straightforward calendar.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Routine vs TickTick
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"TickTick gives you a simple task manager that works without confusion, while Routine tries to do too much and ends up feeling messy.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Routine if you really need its mix of planning and databases, but TickTick is the pick if you just want something that handles tasks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Safari vs Chromium
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Safari gives you a reliable, familiar experience with good battery life and smooth syncing if you use Apple devices, while Chromium is just the raw codebase with none of the features or polish found in the browsers built from it.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Chromium if you have a specific need to tinker with browser foundations; otherwise, Safari is the practical choice.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Safari vs Edge
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Safari gives you a stable, familiar experience with great battery life and smooth syncing if you use all Apple devices, while Edge wraps some basic AI features but still feels like Microsoft trying to catch up.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Edge if you really want that Copilot AI wrapper; otherwise, Safari is the more reliable and less frustrating pick.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Safari vs Firefox
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Safari gives you a stable, familiar experience with great battery life and smooth syncing if you use all Apple devices, while Firefox trades support and site compatibility for privacy and a less popular extension ecosystem.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Firefox if privacy is your absolute priority; stick with Safari for reliability, especially if you use Apple gear.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Safari vs Opera
tldr: {"root":{"type":"root","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Safari gives you steady battery life and smooth syncing if you use only Apple devices, while Opera just adds some flashy visuals and copies Arc's features without real change.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0},{"type":"paragraph","format":"","indent":0,"version":1,"children":[{"mode":"normal","text":"Only use Safari if you want stability and use Apple gear, but skip Opera since it just recycles its old browser with minor tweaks.","type":"text","style":"","detail":0,"format":0,"version":1}],"direction":null,"textStyle":"","textFormat":0}],"direction":null}}

name: Safari vs Shift
